<!-- agent-py-sidecar: auto-generated; do not delete this line to allow refresh -->
# backend/routers/monitoring.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-02T06:35:40Z

## Purpose
HOLY monitoring AI router — per-dept job + pipeline health surface.

Surfaces the 4 cron jobs (refresh_data / retrain / accuracy_drift /
analysis_rollup) plus on-demand ML pipeline runs plus dispatched agent
tasks. Composes with global §38 audit + §47 3-probe + §57.6 canonical
fields + §64 per-dept artifact pattern + §65.1 #9 observability triad.

Reads run manifests from:
    data/eval/cron/<job_key>/<run_id>/manifest.json
    data/eval/<dept>/<pipeline>/<run_id>/manifest.json

Endpoints:
    GET /api/v1/holy/monitoring/{dept}
    GET /api/v1/holy/monitoring/{dept}/jobs/{job}/runs?limit=20
    GET /api/v1/holy/monitoring/{dept}/jobs/{job}/runs/{run_id}
    GET /api/v1/holy/monitoring/_global

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import json
- from `_` import logging
- from `_` import os
- from `_` import time
- from `pathlib` import Path
- from `typing` import Any
- from `fastapi` import APIRouter, HTTPException, Query, Request
- from `core.middleware` import current_tenant_id

## Inputs
- Env vars: HOLY_MONITORING_AUDIT_PATH

## Process
### Functions
- `_log_monitoring_access(http_request, endpoint)`: Append one §38.3-shaped read trail row. Best-effort — disk errors
- `_validate_dept(dept)`: Raise 404 if dept not in the HOLY 19 — no info leakage.
- `_validate_job(job)`: Raise 404 if cron job key unknown — no info leakage.
- `_scan_runs(audit_dir, limit)`: Return last `limit` run summaries under audit_dir, newest first.
- `_job_health(job_key)`: Compute liveness/readiness for one cron job per §47 3-probe contract.
- `global_rollup(http_request)`: Cross-dept rollup (executive view).  Same job health, projected per dept.
- `dept_monitoring(http_request, dept)`: Per-dept monitoring summary across all 4 cron jobs.
- `list_runs(http_request, dept, job, limit)`: Recent runs of a cron job (cron audits are global, not per-dept; dept
- `get_run(http_request, dept, job, run_id)`: Full manifest of one cron run.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Per §38.3 + §47.6 + §64.43 #7 — monitoring data is INFRASTRUCTURE telemetry
- (fleet-wide ML pipeline health), NOT tenant-scoped data. But every monitoring
- READ is attributed to the caller's tenant for forensic + SOC2 CC4 auditing.
- The data layout stays dept-scoped; the access trail is tenant-attributed.
- Cron job catalog — keep aligned with backend/workers/celery_app.py beat_schedule.
